heading: Voluntary use of credentialing
label: 1119 Voluntary use of credentialing
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (d)(1)(B), was omitted from the Code.Section 622 of this title

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the TSA Modernization Act and also as part of the FAA Reauthorization Act of 2018, and not as part of the Implementing Recommendations of the 9/11 Commission Act of 2007 which comprises this chapter.
DefinitionFor definition of “Administrator” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under , Transportation.section 1902 of Pub. L. 115–254section 101 of Title 49

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: An applicable individual who is subject to credentialing or a background investigation may satisfy that requirement by obtaining a valid transportation security card.

shall expand the transportation security card program, consistent with , to allow an applicable individual who is subject to credentialing or a background investigation to apply for a transportation security card; andsection 70105 of title 46
may charge reasonable fees, in accordance with , for providing the necessary credentialing and background investigation.section 469(a) of this title

children.heading: Vetting
children.label: c Vetting
children.text: The Administrator shall develop and implement a plan to utilize, in addition to any background check required for initial issue, the Federal Bureau of Investigation’s Rap Back Service and other vetting tools as appropriate, including the No-Fly and Selectee lists, to get immediate notification of any criminal activity relating to any person with a valid transportation security card.

children.heading: Applicable individual who is subject to credentialing or a background investigation
children.label: 1 Applicable individual who is subject to credentialing or a background investigation
A
children.text: because of employment is regulated by the Transportation Security Administration, Department of Transportation, or Coast Guard and is required to have a background records check to obtain a hazardous materials endorsement on a commercial driver’s license issued by a State under ; orsection 5103a of title 49

children.label: B
children.text: is required to have a credential and background records check under section 622(d)(2) of this title at a facility with activities that are regulated by the Transportation Security Administration, Department of Transportation, or Coast Guard.1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: C
children.text: shows no signs of tampering; and2So in original.2

children.label: D
children.text: bears a photograph of the individual representing such card.2
